reasoning: Dominant hue is a warm sandy tan/khaki — correct bright outdoor-wall base, strongly Hyrule-reminiscent. Pink/coral accent pixels are palette-consistent (candy-pink present in Echoes swatch). Brick mortar uses lighter cream tones, inter-brick shadows shift to deeper warm brown rather than pure black — shadow hue quality is good. However, scattered blue-grey pixels throughout the brickwork read as slightly cold/grey rather than decisively teal-shifted; they are sparse enough not to dominate but numerous enough to introduce a cool note that weakens overall warmth. Saturation is appropriate for a stone/masonry outdoor wall — not muddy or neon. Internal tone count is roughly 6-8 which is normal for a tile. Palette compatibility is mostly solid; the borderline issue is those blue-grey pixels which sit at the edge of acceptable teal-accent vs. cold-grey deviation. Raising to pass would require those cool pixels to shift warmer or into a clear teal/muted-cobalt that sits on the Echoes palette.
